title: semantic debugger — breakpoints on machine behavior, not code lines
manager: A debugger that breaks on what the machine means. Set a breakpoint on 'about to enter state X,' 'this guard is about to fail,' or 'this transition would be blocked,' and the editor halts the running machine right there and shows the tape, the extended state, and why the step is happening. Ordinary breakpoints live in the host program and know nothing about FSL semantics; this one speaks the machine's own vocabulary, so a modeler who has never opened the generated JavaScript can still step, inspect, and diagnose a stuck machine. It is the interactive twin of the era's forensic verbs — where why_rejected answers after the fact, the debugger lets you watch the rejection happen.
cs: editor/wc tooling over the M3 replay + time-travel surface (W8.22); breakpoints are semantic predicates (state-entry / guard-eval / would-block) rather than source lines.
